pr.title: Split out chunk of ef and remaining ft code from the text sections
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly organization PR that split a chunk of effect-library code and remaining fighter code out of broad asm/melee/text_N.s buckets into subsystem/address-named assembly files, while preserving build order through obj_files.mk. The diff is mostly mechanical relocation of existing assembly, not C decompilation or symbol naming work.
what_changed: Added asm/melee/ef/code_8005B4B8.s as a new effect-related assembly file with a .text section range beginning at 0x8005B4B8 and an inline marker '#Start of eflib.c'.
Added asm/melee/ft/code_800679B0.s as a large new fighter-related assembly file, apparently split from the old generic text section buckets.
Moved large blocks out of asm/melee/text_3.s, asm/melee/text_4.s, and asm/melee/text_6.s; added/rebalanced content in asm/melee/text_5.s and introduced asm/melee/text_7.s.

Updated obj_files.mk so the new ef/code_8005B4B8.o and ft/code_800679B0.o objects are included immediately after text_3.o, and shifted later generic text object references so text_5.o, text_6.o, and text_7.o remain in the intended link order.
No PR body, comments, reviews, or review feedback were present in the provided context.
smart_moves: Split generic text-section assembly into subsystem directories instead of leaving all unknown code in monolithic text_N.s files, making future decompilation searches and ownership easier.
Named new assembly files by subsystem plus starting address, e.g. ef/code_8005B4B8.s and ft/code_800679B0.s, which is useful when real source filenames or function names are not yet known.
Adjusted obj_files.mk in the same PR as the asm split, preserving linker/object ordering after moving code between files.
Kept explicit .section .text address-range comments and existing func_/lbl_ symbols, reducing ambiguity for future matching work.
decomp_lessons: For pre-decomp organization, moving address-contiguous asm into subsystem/address-named files can create more useful work units without requiring any C matching yet.

When splitting assembly out of text_N.s, obj_files.mk ordering is the critical part: the moved object must be inserted where its address range belongs, and later generic text buckets may need to be renumbered or shifted.
Large mechanical asm relocations should preserve labels, global declarations, address comments, and instruction bytes so future symbol recovery can be grounded in the original addresses.

Inline provenance markers such as '#Start of eflib.c' are valuable when a chunk boundary corresponds to a suspected original source file, but the evidence here only shows the marker, not a full source reconstruction.
naming_conventions: Unknown assembly split files use subsystem directory plus code_<start-address>.s, as in asm/melee/ef/code_8005B4B8.s and asm/melee/ft/code_800679B0.s.
Unidentified functions retain func_<address> names, for example func_8005B4B8, func_8005B704, and func_8005B780.
Unidentified labels and data references retain lbl_<address> names, for example lbl_80458EB0, lbl_803C025C, lbl_804D64EC, and lbl_8005B6E0.
The build object paths mirror the asm paths under $(BUILD_DIR), e.g. $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/ef/code_8005B4B8.o.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mechanical asm split: relocate address-contiguous text blocks into new .s files while preserving instruction listings and labels.
Retain .include "macros.inc" at the top of new assembly files.
Retain explicit section range comments such as '.section .text  # 0x8005B4B8 - 0x800679AC' to document the exact address span.
Use obj_files.mk to maintain the object link sequence after splitting monolithic text_N.s files.
Keep calls to known or partially known symbols, such as HSD_ObjAllocInit, while leaving unknown callees as func_<address>.
searchable_terms: Split out chunk of ef and remaining ft code from the text sections
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or rename functions in asm/melee/ef/code_8005B4B8.s.
Find later PRs that replace asm/melee/ft/code_800679B0.s with C fighter code.
Check whether the '#Start of eflib.c' marker was later confirmed by source filename recovery.
Review subsequent obj_files.mk changes around text_5.o, text_6.o, and text_7.o for additional text bucket splits.
